t_ch: 这正是我希望与你单独聊聊的原因。
t_en: That's exactly why I wanted to talk to you in private.

t_ch: 身为没有记忆的幽灵，我对这些痛苦的过往，只能像个看客一样借助你们重新经历，但背负着执念的我却非常理解珀葵，也真的在为这个故事动容。
t_en: As a ghost without memories, I can only experience this painful past as a spectator with your help. But, as I'm carrying the weight of this obsession, I've come to truly understand Bequea. I'm deeply moved by her story.

t_ch: 你能明白吗？在信件里看见陌生人称呼我「亲爱的小珀葵」时，我眼睛真的会泛酸，火箭热烈而真诚的爱，也会让我心口塌陷下软软的一块。
t_en: You know what I mean? When I see strangers addressing me as "Dear Little Bequea" in letters, my eyes will actually start to well up. Rocket's passionate and sincere love also carved a soft hollow in my heart.

t_ch: 而现在，那个等候在过去的火箭，正让我无比愧疚。我想我应该去告诉它，它不是和痛苦一起丢掉的垃圾，它是珀葵错过的家人。
t_en: And now, the Rocket who waited in the past fills me with guilt. I should go back, I think. Go back and tell Rocket that he isn't some trash thrown out with all the pain of the past. Rocket is a family member that Bequea missed out on.

t_ch: 但如你所见，现在的我，已经是一个彻底的幻造种幽灵了。
t_en: But as you can see, I've become a complete imagenae ghost now.

t_ch: 我不知道我还能否承担起小狗沉甸甸的爱。我不知道，那只等待了很久的小狗，想要成为家人的对象，到底还是不是我？
t_en: I don't know if I can handle the heavy weight of that little dog's love anymore. I don't know if I'm still the one that little dog, who has waited for so long, wants to become family with.

t_ch: （接下来我的回答，也许会完全改变她的人生。还是慎重作答吧。）
t_en: (My answer to this might completely change her life. I should choose my words carefully.)

t_ch: 「珀葵」就是珀葵。
t_en: "Bequea" is still Bequea.

t_ch: 我明白了。承诺就是承诺，哪怕历经漫长的等待，也只会变得更加不容辜负。
t_en: I understand. A promise is a promise, and even after a long wait, it only becomes more unforgivable to break.

t_ch: 你只是你自己。
t_en: You... are only yourself.

t_ch: 我明白了。就像《灵物保护法》所规定的那样，幻造种是独立的个体，我无法欺骗自己，取代它真正的家人。
t_en: I understand. As stipulated in Faekind Protection Act, imagenae are independent beings, and I can't deceive myself by trying to take the place of his true family.

t_ch: 回去吧，我知道该怎么做了。
t_en: Let's go back. I know what to do now.

t_ch: 所以哪怕一切虚幻的泡影和簇拥的人群都已消散，珀葵也无力去发现——
t_en: And so, even after all the fleeting mirages and the adoring crowds have faded away, Bequea no longer had the strength to find out...

t_ch: 火箭，谢谢你和它的付出。珀葵做完手术后，真的变得特别幸福。
t_en: Rocket, thank you both for your dedication. After Bequea's surgery, she's become truly happy.

t_ch: （真的吗，那太好了！）
t_en: (Really? That's amazing!)

t_ch: 但你知道我为什么会诞生吗？因为她很愧疚，手术带来的创伤让她把火箭遗忘了，可即便只有本能，她也一直希望能回来，履行成为家人的约定。
t_en: But do you know why I came into existence? Because she felt so guilty. The trauma from the surgery made her forget about Rocket, but even with just instinct alone, she always wanted to come back and fulfill her promise of becoming family.

t_ch: 就像你一直守候着这里一样，我现在也必须回到过去与火箭重逢。所以，你愿意解开对电视机的封锁吗？
t_en: Just as you've been waiting here, I must go back to the past to reunite with Rocket. So, would you be willing to unlock the TV?

t_ch: 「珀葵」…还是要回去吗？
t_en: "Bequea"... Are you still going back?

t_ch: 因为那里有珀葵的家人。
t_en: Because Bequea's family is there.

t_ch: （回到过去，会让你更幸福吗？）
t_en: (Would going back to the past make you happier?)

t_ch: 回到过去，那才是我真正的幸福。
t_en: Going back to the past, that's my true happiness.

t_ch: （…那就回去吧！虽然坚强的话说了很多，但狗其实一直在等你汪！）
t_en: (...Let's head back then! Even though Dog went on with all that tough talk, Dog has been waiting for you all along, woof!)

t_ch: （糟糕，电量警告——）
t_en: (Oh no, low battery warning!)

t_ch: 没关系，这一次的{RUBY_B#重逢}告别{RUBY_E#}，我会用心听你说。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll listen to every word you say during our {RUBY_B#reunion}farewell{RUBY_E#} this time.

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 再见，火箭。
t_en: Goodbye, Rocket.

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 闭上眼睡一觉吧……
t_en: Close your eyes and sleep...

t_ch: 等你醒来，我们已在过去重逢。
t_en: When you wake up, we would have already reunited in the past.

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: 火箭，谢谢你和它的付出。珀葵做完手术后，确实找到了自己真正的幸福。
t_en: Rocket, thank you both for your dedication. After Bequea's surgery, she's found her true happiness.

t_ch: （真的吗，那太好了！）
t_en: (Really? That's amazing!)

t_ch: 所以我也在想，作为幽灵珀葵的我，真正的幸福又是什么。
t_en: So I've been wondering too, what true happiness means for me, a ghost named Bequea.

t_ch: 在遇见你之前，我想要的是摆脱孤单，找到自己存在的理由，而现在，一切的答案都汇聚向了你，我的小狗火箭。
t_en: Before I met you, all I wanted was to escape my loneliness and find a reason for my existence. But now, all the answers lead to you, my little dog, Rocket.

t_ch: 我承诺给你买小狗玩具不是玩笑，说想给你一个家也不是空谈，你是第一个陪伴我的生命，也将是我诞生在这个世界开始，选择的第一个家人。
t_en: My promise to buy you puppy toys wasn't a joke, saying that I wanted to give you a home wasn't just empty talk. You're the first living being to ever keep me company, and you'll be the first family I've chosen since I began existing in this world.

t_ch: 火箭，我可以给你一个家吗？
t_en: Rocket, can I give you a home?

t_ch: 「珀葵」…要放弃执念了吗？
t_en: "Bequea"... Are you giving up your obsession?

t_ch: 因为这里也有守候着我的家人。
t_en: Because my family is waiting for me here too.

t_ch: （我不会让你记起痛苦吗？）
t_en: (Won't I make you remember your pain?)

t_ch: 你会让我得到真正的幸福，不愿意吗？
t_en: You can help me find true happiness. Aren't you willing to help me?

t_ch: （不，我不知道怎么做……）
t_en: (That's not it... I don't know what to do...)

t_ch: （人，成为家人要干什么？要按爪印吗？如果成为家人必须要有一个年限，小狗想要…想要一万年！）
t_en: (Human, what does one need to become family? Do I need to stamp my paw print somewhere? If being family must come with an expiry date, Dog wants it to be... wants it to be ten thousand years!)

t_ch: （糟糕，电量警告——）
t_en: (Oh no, low battery warning!)

t_ch: 汪汪！汪！汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Woof! Woof woof!

t_ch: …它…应该不会话痨一万年的，对吧？
t_en: ...It... won't keep yapping for ten thousand years, right?

t_ch: 我计划之后带火箭离开这个地方。真的很感谢你们的帮助，希望我们以后还有缘能够再见。火箭，该道别了！
t_en: I plan to leave this place with Rocket later. Thank you so much for your help, I hope our paths will cross again someday. Rocket, time to say goodbye!

t_ch: 汪汪——汪！
t_en: Rrr—ruff ruff... Woof!

t_ch: 汪汪汪！汪！
t_en: Woof woof woof! Woof!

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: 请相信，我们会在未来重逢。
t_en: Have faith, we'll meet again in the future.

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...

t_ch: 终于找到你们了，给你发的消息怎么不回？
t_en: I've finally found you guys! Why didn't you answer my messages?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，好久不见。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, long time no see!

t_ch: 佩佩如导弹般冲向艾丝妲，嘤嘤叫唤着，像是在说「我好想你，我好想你！」
t_en: Peppy shoots toward Asta like a missile, squealing with joy as if saying "I missed you so, so, so much!"

t_ch: 我和佩佩在二相乐园很想你！
t_en: Peppy and I missed you so much in Planarcadia!

t_ch: 我也是！
t_en: I did too!

t_ch: 可恨电视机里没有信号…
t_en: Dang it, there was no signal in the TV...

t_ch: 看来你们去了一些有趣的地方冒险。
t_en: Looks like you've been adventuring in some interesting places.

t_ch: 我刚刚很担心呢，因为佩佩的装备不明缘由突然电量快速下降…原来是打开了扩大同步范围的特殊模式，难怪。
t_en: I was really worried just now because Peppy's equipment was losing power rapidly for no apparent reason... Oh, the expanded sync range mode is switched on, that explains it.

t_ch: 真是谢谢你的照顾了，我的购物计划还有第二场，有什么想要的，也列个清单给我吧。至于佩佩…既然事件已经解决，它也该回家吃狗粮了。
t_en: Thank you so much for looking after him for me! I still have a second round of shopping to do, so if there's anything you want, just give me a list. As for Peppy... Now that this matter is resolved, it's about time he comes home and has some food.

t_ch: 在你的目送下，佩佩泪眼婆娑地舔了舔艾丝妲，回到了它心爱的家人身边。
t_en: Under your watchful gaze, Peppy gives Asta a tearful lick before returning to his beloved family.

t_ch: 你们一定要做一辈子的家人啊！
t_en: You must stay family for life!

t_ch: 你俩把日子过好比什么都重要！
t_en: You two! Just live your best life! That's what matters most!

t_ch: 你们到底经历了什么啊？？？
t_en: What on earth did you all go through?

t_ch: 等佩佩把电充满，我可一定要听听。
t_en: Once Peppy is fully charged, I must hear about this.

t_ch: 那我先带佩佩回住处了。{NICKNAME}，等有时间我请你吃饭～拜拜！
t_en: I'll take Peppy back to our accommodations first. {NICKNAME}, let me treat you to a meal when I have time~ Bye-bye!

t_ch: 你再度回到熟悉的地方，见到了一束不知何人放下的花。泛黄的照片上她笑容灿烂，但愿她与她的家人，不再离散……
t_en: You return to this familiar place and find a bouquet of flowers left by an unknown person. In the yellowed photograph, her smile is radiant. May she and her family never be separated again...

t_ch: 永远幸福。
t_en: And be happy forever.

t_ch: 你再度回到熟悉的地方，见到了一束不知何人放下的花。小小的狗爪印点缀一旁，看来她并非孤身一人，但愿她们自此不再离散……
t_en: You return to this familiar place and find a bouquet of flowers left by an unknown person. Tiny little paw prints adorn its side. Looks like she wasn't alone after all. May they never be separated again...

t_ch: 永远幸福。
t_en: And be happy forever.

t_ch: （嗯？路边有只小狗…怎么感觉背影很眼熟……）
t_en: (Hm? There's a little dog by the road... Why does its silhouette look so familiar...)

t_ch: （过去看看吧。）
t_en: (Take a closer look.)

t_ch: 总感觉，有些在意他们口中的「那件事」……
t_en: I can't help but feel a bit bothered by this "incident" they speak of...

t_ch: （是秘密的气息，汪！）
t_en: (It's the scent of secrets, woof!)

t_ch: （还在录制吗？真辛苦呀！）
t_en: (Are we still recording? Man, this is tough!)

t_ch: 看起来是在节目后采……
t_en: Looks like it's a post-show interview...

t_ch: 喜欢和我玩吗？给你买点玩具好不好？
t_en: Do you like playing with me? Shall I buy you some toys?

t_ch: 汪！汪汪——汪！
t_en: Woof! Woof woof—woof!

t_ch: （果然…火箭就是那个「他」，而我现在就是火箭……）
t_en: (Just as I thought... Rocket is "him," and I'm Rocket now...)

t_ch: 珀葵…幸福……
t_en: Bequea... happiness...

t_ch: 追上…珀葵…家人……
t_en: Catch up... Bequea... Family...

t_ch: 追不上…珀葵……
t_en: Can't catch up... Bequea...

t_ch: 珀葵…痛苦…别追…痛苦！
t_en: Bequea... Pain... Stop chasing... pain!

t_ch: 再见…珀葵……
t_en: Goodbye... Bequea...

t_ch: 在此等待一会儿吧
t_en: Stay here for a bit

t_ch: 桥依小姐！桥依小姐！你一定不能有事啊！呜呜呜…对不起，我们没有保护好你……
t_en: Miss Joye! Miss Joye! Please, hang in there! *sob*... I'm sorry, we failed to protect you...

t_ch: 别担心啦～桥依可是很难杀的！难道我桥依身为「地表基础最扎实的偶像」，还需要你们来保护吗？别自责了，乖～
t_en: Don't worry~ This pretty little bridge isn't that easy to kill! As "The Idol with the Strongest Foundation Above Ground," do you really think I need protection? No need to beat yourself up about it, sweetie~

t_ch: 你怎么在和桥对话？
t_en: Why are you talking to a bridge?

t_ch: 你这话什么意思？她可不是一般的「桥」啊！这是我们二维市首屈一指的偶像，桥依小姐！
t_en: Excuse you! What are you trying to say? She's not just any "bridge"! This is Miss Joye, the top underground idol in all of Duomension City!

t_ch: 发生什么事了吗？
t_en: Did something happen?

t_ch: 桥依小姐被人袭击了！你难道看不出来吗？桥依小姐那双漂亮的眼睛被人夺走了！
t_en: Miss Joye was attacked, obviously! Can't you tell? Look! Her beautiful eyes were stolen!

t_ch: 所以…这座桥叫做桥依？
t_en: So... this bridge is called Joye?

t_ch: 整个二相乐园还有哪座桥叫桥依？桥依小姐遇袭，我们都快急死了，无关人士就不要添乱了好吗！
t_en: Is there any other Joye in all of Planarcadia? Miss Joye was attacked! We're all losing our minds here, so if you're not part of this, then stay out of our way, will ya!?

t_ch: 丝葵德小姐！我理解你着急的心情，但是不可以对别人失礼哦！《桥依粉丝手册》怎么写的来着？
t_en: Miss Skuid! I understand you're anxious, but you can't be rude to others! Do you remember what the Joye Fan Manual has to say about that?

t_ch: 呜呜呜…「任何路人都是桥依小姐的潜在粉丝」…对不起，桥依小姐。对不起，这位潜在粉丝。我实在太心急了，才会那么凶。
t_en: *sob* *sniff*... "Strangers are just fans Miss Joye hasn't met yet"... I'm sorry, Miss Joye. I'm sorry, potential fan. I just lost my cool because I was in such a rush.

t_ch: 你好，{NICKNAME}，我是二维市的一个「偶像」，叫做「桥依」。初次见面…哦，不算初次见面了，你刚到二维市的时候，就从我身上路过过一次～我还记得呢！
t_en: Hi, {NICKNAME}. I'm a Duomension City "idol," and my name is Joye. Nice to meet you... Oh! Well, it's not quite our first meeting. You actually passed on me once when you first arrived in Duomension City~ I haven't forgotten, you know!

t_ch: 旁边这位是我粉丝会的会长，丝葵德小姐。她也是个很好的人啦，只是太担心我，才出言冒犯你，请原谅她吧。
t_en: And this here is Miss Skuid, the president of my fan club. She's a good person, really! She just got a bit carried away because she's worried about me. Please forgive her.

t_ch: 过街天桥是偶像？
t_en: A pedestrian overpass is an idol?

t_ch: 二相乐园很神奇吧？在二相乐园，任何生灵都可以尝试成为偶像，即使是一座桥。
t_en: Isn't Planarcadia amazing? In Planarcadia, any living being can try to become an idol, even a bridge!

t_ch: 重量级偶像啊！
t_en: Whoa! Not just any idol, but an absolute heavyweight!

t_ch: 不许这么说桥依！要说也该说是…「最不会翻车的偶像」！
t_en: Don't you talk about me like that! If anything, I'm... "The idol least likely to flop!"

t_ch: 虽然很希望能让你现在立刻马上成为桥依的新粉丝啦…但我刚刚遭遇了袭击，双眼被不明歹徒摘取了。
t_en: As much as I'd love to make you my newest fan right here and now... I was just attacked, and the culprit stole my eyes. I haven't the foggiest who did it.

t_ch: 现在的桥依，属于重伤状态…如果可以的话，等我康复之后，再来看我吧～
t_en: So currently I'm in a grievously injured state... If possible, please do come visit me again after I've recovered, okay~?

t_ch: 桥依小姐，都这种时候了，就不要硬撑着偶像营业了。
t_en: Miss Joye, at a time like this, you needn't try too hard to maintain your idol persona!

t_ch: 我是「幻造医师」因巡，请问伤者在哪里？
t_en: I'm Izol, an imagenesis doctor. Where's the patient?

t_ch: 因巡医生，您终于来了！我真急死了！伤者就是这边这位，桥依小姐。您看看她情况严重不严重。
t_en: Dr. Izol, you're finally here! I've been worried sick! The patient is Miss Joye here. Please, could you assess how critical her condition is?

t_ch: 潜在粉丝{F#小姐}{M#先生}，不好意思，请先让一让。医生要对桥依小姐进行急救措施了。
t_en: Excuse me, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Potential Fan, please step aside. The doctor needs to give Miss Joye emergency care! Immediately!

t_ch: 呜…桥依小姐的情况还好吗？
t_en: *sniff*... How's Miss Joye? How bad is it?

t_ch: 创伤本身问题不是很大。这位袭击者的幻造技术很高超啊，没伤到其余的身体结构，只是摘除了双眼。
t_en: The injury itself isn't too serious. The attacker's imagenesis technique was superb. They didn't damage any other bodily structures and just removed both eyes.

t_ch: 只要稍微做一些应急处理，桥依小姐应该*暂时*不会有什么生命危险。
t_en: With some emergency treatment, Miss Joye shouldn't be in any life-threatening danger... for the moment.

t_ch: 谢谢你，因巡医生。但是…桥依还是觉得，自己现在很虚弱。
t_en: Thank you, Dr. Izol. But... I... still feel very weak right now.

t_ch: 唉…那当然会虚弱。你身上九成的愿力都凝聚在那双眼睛上吧？粉丝们平常所关注的也都是那双眼睛吧？
t_en: *sigh*... That's natural. <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of your Wishpower is concentrated in those eyes of yours, isn't it? Of course you'd feel weak. Those eyes of yours are usually all your fans pay attention to anyway, right?

t_ch: 你被夺取了九成的愿力。如果一个人类迅速失去了九成的体重，应该是立刻就会死亡的。而你现在的情况，和那差不了多少。
t_en: <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of your Wishpower has been stolen. If a human suddenly lost <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of their body weight, they'd die instantly. And your current situation isn't much different from that.

t_ch: 你想想办法呀因巡医生！你的诊所是二维市最好的诊所，肯定有办法能救救桥依小姐的吧？求求你了！救救桥依小姐！
t_en: Please, think of something, Dr. Izol! Your clinic is the best in Duomension City. Surely there must be a way to save Miss Joye? I'm begging you! Please save Miss Joye!

t_ch: 丝葵德小姐，冷静一点。桥依还需要你做更重要的事呢！
t_en: Miss Skuid, I must insist that you calm down. I still need you to do something more important for me!

t_ch: 请你回到粉丝会，帮桥依告诉大家，桥依只是受了些伤，没什么大碍，请大家安心等待桥依归来～拜托你啦！
t_en: Please go back to the fan club and let everyone know that I've been hurt, but that it's nothing serious. Tell them not to worry and wait patiently for my return~ I'm counting on you, okay~?

t_ch: 呜呜呜…我明白了，我这就回去…桥依小姐，你要信守诺言，一定要回来啊！
t_en: *sniff*... I understand. I'll head back now... Miss Joye, you have to keep your promise! You have to be okay, you hear me!?

t_ch: 因巡医生…桥依不能就这么死了，你也看到了，还有很多人在等我。
t_en: Dr. Izol... I can't die like this. You saw it yourself. There are still so many people waiting for me.

t_ch: 唉…以你现在的状况，幻造结构是随时可能崩解的。
t_en: *sigh*... In your current condition, your imagenated structure could disintegrate at any moment.

t_ch: 想要维持形态，有两个办法——要么你尽快将愿力补充到遇袭前的水平，要么你找回自己丢失的愿力。
t_en: If you want to maintain your form, you've got two options. Either you find a way ASAP to restore your Wishpower to the level it was at before you were attacked, or you recover your lost Wishpower.

t_ch: 哈哈，听起来都不是很简单呀……
t_en: Haha, neither option sounds very easy...

t_ch: 各位现场人员请注意，我是朽叶，这里现在由异常防御部接管。本案及现场的调查工作将由我方接手，请各单位配合。
t_en: Attention, everyone, attention! I'm Kuchiba, and the Department of Aberration Defense will now be taking over the investigation of this case and the crime scene. We expect and appreciate your cooperation. Thank you!

t_ch: 桥依小姐，方便谈谈发生了什么吗？
t_en: Now, Miss Joye, would you mind telling me what happened?

t_ch: 朽叶小姐…坦白说，桥依也不太清楚发生了什么…我毕竟是一座过街天桥嘛，平时身上人来人往的，不太可能专门注意到谁。
t_en: Miss Kuchiba... to be honest, I'm not too sure about what happened either... I mean, at the end of the day, I'm just a pedestrian overpass, so people are constantly walking all over me, and it's not like I could pay attention to anyone in particular.

t_ch: 今天像平常一样，桥依一边守护着过桥的行人，一边悄悄练习最近要唱的歌。有个蒙面人朝着桥依走来，桥依也没放在心上。毕竟咱们这里怪人多得很。
t_en: It was a day like any other day, and I was quietly practicing my new song while watching over the pedestrians crossing me. A masked person approached, but I thought nothing of it. After all, there are plenty of weirdos around here.

t_ch: 没想到…这人突然掏出绘具——就是绘师用来创造幻造种的那些尺规之类的，朝我挥舞了起来，再之后…我眼前就一片漆黑了。
t_en: But then... they suddenly whipped out their drawing tools — you know, those compasses and rulers that artists use to create imagenae and stuff — and started waving them at me. After that... everything went pitch black before my eyes.

t_ch: 我明白了。桥依小姐有怀疑的对象吗？最近是否得罪了什么人？
t_en: I see. Is there anyone you suspect, Miss Joye? Have you made any enemies recently?

t_ch: …桥依确实没什么头绪。我并不是会得罪人的性格，也还没火到有黑粉的程度。要说是竞争对手，那更不可能，桥依这小众赛道，谁没事会和我竞争啊！
t_en: ...I really haven't a clue. I'm not the type to make enemies, and I'm definitely not famous enough to have haters yet. As for rivals? That's even more unlikely! My niche is super specific. Who would possibly want to compete with me?

t_ch: 嗯…我想也是。
t_en: Hmm... I suppose that's true enough.

t_ch: 那你对凶手的身份有头绪吗？是人类还是幻造种，是什么种族、什么性别、什么身高体型，任何事都可以。
t_en: In that case, do you have any idea about your attacker's identity? Are they human or imagenae? Any clues about their race, gender, height, build... anything at all would help.

t_ch: 对不起…一切发生得太突然了，桥依没来得及仔细观察。
t_en: Sorry... it all happened so fast, I didn't get a chance to take a good look.

t_ch: 没关系，请不必自责。
t_en: It's alright, there's no need to blame yourself.

t_ch: 因巡医生，听你刚才的意思，桥依小姐现在的状况比较危险？我直说好了：她还能撑多久？
t_en: Dr. Izol, what you said before... is Miss Joye's condition critical? Or, if I may be blunt: How much longer can she hold on?

t_ch: 我不知道。幻造种的愿力结构是个混沌体系，并不像物质生命那样井然有序。运气好的话，她会渡过难关，顺利活下去；运气不好的话…几个系统时内，她就会彻底崩解。
t_en: I can't say for sure. The Wishpower structure of imagenae is a chaotic system, not orderly like material life forms. If she's lucky, she'll make it through and survive. If she isn't... she'll completely disintegrate within a few system hours.

t_ch: 但无论「幸运」与否，短时间内遭受大量构造性愿力缺失，都会造成不可逆的影响。如果不能补全这些愿力，桥依小姐的记忆、人格、技能…全部都会受到严重的损伤。
t_en: But regardless of "luck," suffering a massive loss of constructive Wishpower in such a short time will cause irreversible damage. If her Wishpower isn't replenished, Miss Joye's memories, personality, and abilities... Everything will be severely harmed.

t_ch: 除非…桥依能找回自己的眼睛。
t_en: Unless... I can get my eyes back.

t_ch: 这的确是最好的解决方案…但我们都不知道袭击者是谁，也不知道此人要用桥依小姐的眼睛做什么。这恐怕很难吧。
t_en: That's definitely the best solution... but we don't even know who the attacker is or what they want with her eyes. I'm afraid this is going to be a tough one.

t_ch: 确实会很难，但好人要是能轻易战胜坏人，还要我们异常防御部做什么？接下来的事，就交给我们处理吧。
t_en: It'll be tough, for sure. But if it were that easy for the good guys to defeat the bad guys, what would be the point of having us at the Department of Aberration Defense? Leave the rest to us.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你看起来对这件事很感兴趣？有兴趣来帮个忙吗？
t_en: Looks like this incident has piqued your interest, {NICKNAME}. Care to lend a hand?

t_ch: 怎么突然有我的事？
t_en: Me? Why am I suddenly getting dragged into this?

t_ch: 你可是重要外宾，怎么能不让你有些参与感呢？
t_en: You're an important guest! How could we possibly let you just sit on the sidelines?

t_ch: 参与异常防御部办案，不合规矩吧？
t_en: Getting involved in a Department of Aberration Defense case... Isn't that against protocol?

t_ch: 异常防御部向来不拘小节。我们要是桩桩件件事都守规矩，破案率起码要下降一半。
t_en: The Department of Aberration Defense has always been flexible with the rules. If we followed every single little rule and did everything by the book, our case clearance rate would drop by at least half.

t_ch: 我可以拒绝吗？
t_en: Can I say no?

t_ch: 你无意帮忙，却在犯罪现场偷听了那么久的现场调查，我只好以妨碍公务之名把你抓回去了…开个玩笑。
t_en: So you don't want to help, but you do want to eavesdrop on sensitive case information being discussed at a crime scene investigation. I should arrest you for obstruction of justice... Just kidding.

t_ch: 如果你愿意帮忙，就过来和我们一起聊聊吧。
t_en: If you decide you'd like to help, come on over and talk with us.

t_ch: 现在的情况是比较危急的。桥依小姐遭到重创，如果不能尽快找回她的眼睛，她的健康恐怕会受到相当严重的影响。
t_en: At this time, the situation is critical. Miss Joye has suffered a grave injury, and if we can't find her eyes soon, I'm afraid her health will be seriously affected.

t_ch: 我能帮什么忙？
t_en: How can I help?

t_ch: 我可是听说，「球棒浣熊」相当擅长处理这种危急的局面。
t_en: Word is that the "Baseball Raccoon" is quite adept at handling sticky situations like this.

t_ch: 我来负责募捐愿力吧！
t_en: I'll handle collecting Wishpower!

t_ch: 这种事情交给「球棒浣熊」来做，恐怕有些大材小用了吧。
t_en: Having the "Baseball Raccoon" handle something like this might be a bit like using a sledgehammer to crack a nut.

t_ch: 现在的当务之急，自然是尽快找到袭击桥依小姐的凶手，寻回她被夺取的双眼。
t_en: But right now, our top priority is to quickly track down the culprit who attacked Miss Joye and recover her stolen eyes.

t_ch: 不过，这里不是详谈的地方，我们找个方便说话的场所，再仔细聊聊这桩案子吧。
t_en: However, this isn't the place to be discussing it. Let's find somewhere more private to go over the details.

t_ch: 我们出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 这附近有个茶室，我们去那里聊吧。
t_en: There's a tearoom nearby. Let's talk there.

t_ch: 请带上我一起去吧。
t_en: Please, take me with you.

t_ch: 茶室装得下过街天桥吗？
t_en: Can an overhead pedestrian bridge fit in a tearoom?

t_ch: 讨厌啦！桥依也不会把自己整个塞进茶室啦！
t_en: Hey, don't say things like that! It's not like I'd try to squeeze my entire self into the room!

t_ch: 过街天桥还能移动吗？
t_en: Can a pedestrian overpass even move?

t_ch: 桥依要是满街乱跑，会造成交通事故吧！
t_en: Were I to run around the streets, I'd probably cause a traffic accident!

t_ch: 桥依好歹也是偶像，偶尔也要去Livehouse的。像我们这样不方便移动的幻造种，普遍都能够将愿力灌注在一些部分，让它可以暂时脱离本体。美食街的克拉肯先生也可以哦！
t_en: I am an idol, you know! That means I have to go to perform at live venues and the like. For imagenae like me who can't move around easily, we can generally channel our Wishpower into certain parts to temporarily detach them from our main body. Kraken from the food street can do it, too!

t_ch: 补充一句，桥依小姐的眼睛是被凶手用绘具从本体上强行「剥离」的。和这种自主离开本体的行为有很大的差别。
t_en: I'd like to point out that Miss Joye's eyes were forcibly "peeled" off her body by the killer using art tools. That's very different from voluntarily separating parts of your body.

t_ch: 但是，桥依小姐，你现在的身体状况并不好。在大量缺失愿力的情况下，还要暂时离开本体行动…我强烈建议你不要这么做。
t_en: But Miss Joye, your body is in extremely poor shape right now. Leaving your physical body to act separately after losing such a massive amount of Wishpower... I must strongly advise against such rash behavior.

t_ch: 桥依可不是那种乖乖等待别人拯救的性格！我要参与调查、积极自救，也许能够帮到你们呢。
t_en: I'm not the type to just sit around waiting to be saved! I want to join your investigation and do something! Maybe I'll even be able to help!

t_ch: 那好，我们走吧。桥依小姐，记得不要逞强。
t_en: Very well, Miss Joye, let's go. But remember not to push yourself too hard.

t_ch: 我再准备一下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 请尽快，我在这里等你。
t_en: Time is of the essence. I'll wait for you here.

t_ch: 你们说…桥依小姐，该不会就这么……
t_en: You don't think... Is it possible Miss Joye could...

t_ch: 你少说两句*吉祥话*吧！没人把你当哑巴。
t_en: Would it kill you to just stay quiet? Nobody asked you to jinx anything!

t_ch: 桥依小姐不会有事的，她总能渡过难关。
t_en: Miss Joye will be fine! She always manages to pull through!

t_ch: 我们找个方便说话的场所，再仔细聊聊这桩案子吧。
t_en: Let's find a better place to talk so we can go over the case in detail.

t_ch: 我们出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 这附近有个茶室，我们去那里聊吧。
t_en: There's a tearoom nearby. Let's talk there.

t_ch: 请带上我一起去吧。
t_en: Please, take me with you.

t_ch: 茶室装得下过街天桥吗？
t_en: Can an overhead pedestrian bridge fit in a tearoom?

t_ch: 讨厌啦！桥依也不会把自己整个塞进茶室啦！
t_en: Hey, don't say things like that! It's not like I'd try to squeeze my entire self into the room!

t_ch: 过街天桥还能移动吗？
t_en: Can a pedestrian overpass even move?

t_ch: 桥依要是满街乱跑，会造成交通事故吧！
t_en: Were I to run around the streets, I'd probably cause a traffic accident!

t_ch: 桥依好歹也是偶像，偶尔也要去Livehouse的。像我们这样不方便移动的幻造种，普遍都能够将愿力灌注在一些部分，让它可以暂时脱离本体。美食街的克拉肯先生也可以哦！
t_en: I am an idol, you know! That means I have to go to perform at live venues and the like. For imagenae like me who can't move around easily, we can generally channel our Wishpower into certain parts to temporarily detach them from our main body. Kraken from the food street can do it, too!

t_ch: 补充一句，桥依小姐的眼睛是被凶手用绘具从本体上强行「剥离」的。和这种自主离开本体的行为有很大的差别。
t_en: I'd like to point out that Miss Joye's eyes were forcibly "peeled" off her body by the killer using art tools. That's very different from voluntarily separating parts of your body.

t_ch: 但是，桥依小姐，你现在的身体状况并不好。在大量缺失愿力的情况下，还要暂时离开本体行动…我强烈建议你不要这么做。
t_en: But Miss Joye, your body is in extremely poor shape right now. Leaving your physical body to act separately after losing such a massive amount of Wishpower... I must strongly advise against such rash behavior.

t_ch: 桥依可不是那种乖乖等待别人拯救的性格！我要参与调查、积极自救，也许能够帮到你们呢。
t_en: I'm not the type to just sit around waiting to be saved! I want to join your investigation and do something! Maybe I'll even be able to help!

t_ch: 那好，我们走吧。桥依小姐，记得不要逞强。
t_en: Very well, Miss Joye, let's go. But remember not to push yourself too hard.

t_ch: 我再准备一下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 请尽快，我在这里等你。
t_en: Time is of the essence. I'll wait for you here.

t_ch: 干什么啦！不要一直盯着桥依的嘴唇看啦…就是因为这个部位不好看，我才一直藏在桥梁后面的。
t_en: Hey, stop that! Please don't stare at my lips like that... I've been hiding behind the bridge because I'm self-conscious about them, you know!

t_ch: 还是很可爱的啦。
t_en: Don't worry, you're still adorable!

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，你人真好～
t_en: Hehe, you're such a sweetheart~

t_ch: 主要是漂浮的红唇有点惊悚。
t_en: It's mainly that those floating red lips are kinda creepy.

t_ch: 没办法嘛，眼睛让人夺走了嘛……
t_en: I can't help it. After all, my eyes were taken...

t_ch: 你没有眼睛，怎么知道我盯你？
t_en: You don't even have eyes. How do you know where I'm looking?

t_ch: 桥依能感觉到你的视线啦！
t_en: I can feel your gaze!

t_ch: 还是先来谈谈正事吧。我们直奔主题：异常防御部接手这起案件，是因为桥依小姐此番遭遇的袭击，是一起连续杀人事件的一部分。
t_en: Let's get right down to business. Here's the deal: The Department of Aberration Defense took this case because the attack on Miss Joye is part of a serial murder investigation.

t_ch: 桥依还活着呢！
t_en: Joye is still alive!

t_ch: 桥依可是很难杀的！
t_en: I'm not that easy to kill!

t_ch: 欢愉之地还有这么可怕的案件？
t_en: Do scary things like that really happen in the land of Elation?

t_ch: 很遗憾，二相乐园终究不是乐园。
t_en: Alas, at the end of the day, Planarcadia is no arcadia.

t_ch: 最近几个月，二维市发生了一连串杀人事件——具体来说，是<unbreak>17</unbreak>起…哦，现在是<unbreak>18</unbreak>起了。经过调查，这些案件有着相当多的共通点。
t_en: In recent months, there's been a series of murders in Duomension City. <unbreak>17</unbreak> cases to be exact... oh, make that <unbreak>18</unbreak> now. Our investigation has turned up a number of similarities between the incidents.

t_ch: 具体情况，这里有一份案件简报，你可以自行阅读一下。
t_en: As for the specifics, here's the case summary. You can read through it yourself.

t_ch: 容我稍微解说一下。首先，受害者都是从事演艺工作的幻造种，有主播、歌手、笑星，当然还有桥依小姐这样的偶像。
t_en: Here are some of the main points. First, all the victims were imagenae working in the entertainment industry. Streamers, singers, comedians, and of course, idols like Miss Joye.

t_ch: 其次，所有受害者都被取下了一些身体部件。凶手的幻造技巧相当优秀，「剥离」的手法干净利落，几乎没留下任何可供调查的线索。
t_en: Secondly, every victim had body parts removed. The perpetrator's imagenesis technique is exceptional. Their "peeling" method is so clean and precise, it leaves almost no clues behind for us to follow.

t_ch: 还有最后一个共通点：案发现场都在被害人自己的地盘，住宅、工作室、专属休息室…但完全没有暴力闯入的痕迹。
t_en: And there's one last thing tying everything together: All the crimes happened on the victims' own turf. Homes, studios, private lounges... but there were absolutely no signs of forced entry.

t_ch: 简直就像是「被邀请来的客人」一样。
t_en: Almost as if they were "invited in."

t_ch: 尽管如此，但比较诡异的一点是，所有被害人都没有把「今天有人要来访」这件事告诉任何亲朋好友。
t_en: However, what's strange about that is that none of the victims told any of their family or friends about having "invited a guest" or someone coming to visit.

t_ch: 异常防御部暂时将这位神秘的连环杀手称为「千禧杀手」。因为今年是<unbreak>1999</unbreak>年嘛。
t_en: The Department of Aberration Defense is temporarily calling this mysterious serial killer the "Millennium Killer." After all, it's the year <unbreak>1999</unbreak>.

t_ch: 原来这段时间网上盛传的「千禧杀手」是真的！桥依还以为只是个都市传说呢！
t_en: So the Millennium Killer everyone's been talking about online is real! And here I thought it was just an urban legend!

t_ch: 既然是被邀请来的客人…那应该是被害人都认识的人吧？
t_en: Since they were invited in... The killer must have been known to the victims, right?

t_ch: 在案件被移交到异常防御部之前，治安局就沿着这条思路进行过调查。可是虽然被害人都是演艺行业的，但「歌手」和「笑星」的圈子毕竟还是不同的，「共同好友」寥寥无几。
t_en: Before the case was transferred to the Department of Aberration Defense, the Security Bureau had already investigated along these lines. However, even though all the victims were in the entertainment industry, the circles of "singers" and "comedians" are quite distinct with minimal overlap, so there are few "mutual friends" between them.

t_ch: 排查下来，被害人确实都认识几个电视台的制片人。但这几位制片人都有充足的不在场证明。
t_en: The investigation showed the victims did indeed all know several TV producers. However, all the producers had solid alibis.

t_ch: 先等等…这些「共通点」听起来和桥依遇到的案件不太一样啊？凶手没有来桥依家，而是当街袭击了桥依。况且桥依也没有被杀害，而是活了下来…虽然还是很受伤啦。
t_en: Wait a minute... my case doesn't share any of those "similarities" with the other cases, right? The culprit didn't come to my house, but attacked me right there on the street. Plus, I wasn't killed, I'm still here... though I am badly hurt.

t_ch: 这些差异，恐怕和桥依小姐本身的特性有关。
t_en: As for those differences, it probably comes down to a few of your innate traits.

t_ch: 凶手没办法「拜访你的家」，因为你平时就是驻扎在街上的。凶手也没法「杀害」你，作为一个大体型的幻造种，你的生命力太顽强了——实在是不幸中的万幸。
t_en: The killer can't "visit your home" since you're always out in public. They can't "murder" you either. Being a large imagenae, you're just too tough to kill outright. Talk about a blessing in disguise.

t_ch: 但是，凶手在众目睽睽之下，迅速「剥离」了你的双眼，包括你在内，没有任何目击证人能说清凶手的身份特征…这样卓越又残忍的幻造技艺，恐怕只有「千禧杀手」掌握了。
t_en: However, the killer swiftly "peeled" away your eyes in public. No witness, including you, could describe any of the killer's identifying features... Such an exceptional yet cruel imagenesis technique could only be the work of a master such as the "Millennium Killer."

t_ch: 这也是为什么，我会判断「桥依小姐遇袭事件」同样是「千禧杀手」的手笔。
t_en: Which is to say, that's also why I concluded that "The Joye Ambush Incident" was the work of the Millennium Killer as well.

t_ch: 所以到底为什么找我帮忙？
t_en: So why exactly do you need my help?

t_ch: 是不是告诉我太多机密了？
t_en: Aren't you telling me way too much?

t_ch: 因为「常规」的调查方式，效率太低了。
t_en: Because the "standard" way to investigate is too inefficient.

t_ch: 现在桥依小姐的双眼下落不明，甚至生命都可能危在旦夕，我不能把桥依小姐的健康和未来赌在「稳扎稳打」的调查上。
t_en: Now Miss Joye's eyes are missing, and she is in imminent danger of losing her life. I can't gamble her health and future on a "slow and steady" investigation.

t_ch: 所以，我恳求你，{NICKNAME}，用属于无名客的方式，在「千禧杀手」对那双眼睛做出什么事之前，将它夺回来。可以吗？
t_en: So, I'm begging you, {NICKNAME}, to retrieve the eyes in true Nameless fashion before the "Millennium Killer" does something to them. Can you do that?

t_ch: 没问题，我最喜欢助人为乐了。
t_en: No problem. I love helping out!

t_ch: 太好了，非常符合我对无名客的认识。感谢你愿意出手相助。
t_en: Perfect, that's exactly what I'd expect from a Nameless. Thanks for being willing to lend a hand.

t_ch: 听起来有点违法，得加钱。
t_en: Sounds kinda illegal, so it's gonna cost you extra.

t_ch: 没问题，非常符合我对「球棒浣熊」的认识。请别担心，一切责任由我一人承担。
t_en: No problem. That's no less than I'd expect from the "Baseball Raccoon." Don't worry, I'll be the only party liable for everything that occurs.

t_ch: 那么，这次会面就到此结束。接下来的事，就拜托你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Well then, this meeting is over. I'll leave the rest to you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 朽叶小姐，你不和我们一起调查吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to investigate with us, Miss Kuchiba?

t_ch: 身份所限，很多事就不便直接出手了。但请放心，今天之内，只要{NICKNAME}不做过于出格的事，各地治安官都会对你的调查视而不见。
t_en: Because of my identity, I can't intervene directly in many things. But rest assured, at least for today, so long as {NICKNAME} doesn't do anything too outrageous, security officers all over will turn a blind eye to your "investigation."

t_ch: 我能做到的也只有这些了。{NICKNAME}、桥依小姐，祝你们好运。
t_en: This is all I can do. {NICKNAME}, Miss Joye, good luck to you both.

t_ch: 唉，虽然朽叶小姐提供了内部资料，但我们缺少的信息还是太多了…既然被害人都混演艺圈，那如果能找个业内人士咨询一下就好了。
t_en: *sigh* Even with Miss Kuchiba's inside info, we're still missing way too much information... Since the victims were all working in the entertainment industry, it'd be great if we could consult someone from the industry.

t_ch: 你自己不就是业内人士？
t_en: Aren't you an insider yourself?

t_ch: 桥依这种小偶像算什么「业内人士」啊！至少要上过跨年晚会才算「业内」吧。
t_en: I'm just some small-time idol. How could I be an "industry insider"? You gotta at least perform at the New Year Countdown Party to be considered "in the industry"!

t_ch: 你有什么熟识的人选吗？
t_en: Do you have anyone in mind?

t_ch: 说来丢人，桥依的圈内好友，大部分混得还不如桥依呢…估计是很难提供什么帮助的。
t_en: Though I hate to say it, most of my friends in the industry are doing even worse than I am... I doubt they'll be of much help.

t_ch: 诶？火花算不算业内人士？
t_en: Hmm... Would Sparxie count as an industry insider?

t_ch: 火花…她当然算了！二相乐园粉丝量最高的偶像，圈内人望也不可小觑，如果她愿意提供帮助……
t_en: Sparxie... of course she counts! She's Planarcadia's most followed virtual idol, and her industry prestige is considerable. If she's willing to help...

t_ch: …不过，你不是才打了人家一顿吗？虽然说幻月游戏上相互对抗在所难免，但她该不会因此记恨你，不肯提供帮助吧？
t_en: ...But didn't you just beat her up? Sure, clashing during the Phantasmoon Games is inevitable, but you don't think she'll hold a grudge and refuse to help because of that, do you?

t_ch: 火花是敞亮人，不至于。
t_en: Sparxie's not that kind of person, it'll be fine.

t_ch: 唔…但愿如此吧。
t_en: Hmm... I hope you're right.

t_ch: 只能问问看了…
t_en: Guess we'll have to ask...

t_ch: 希望她不要记仇哇！
t_en: I hope she doesn't hold a grudge!

t_ch: 桥依宝宝！听说你被「千禧杀手」袭击了，好可怜哦！别担心，我们一定会抓住这家伙的！
t_en: Poor Baby Joye! I heard you were attacked by the Millennium Killer, you poor thing! Don't worry, we'll definitely catch this creep!

t_ch: 火…火花小姐竟然认识桥依？
t_en: M—Miss Sparxie knows about me!?

t_ch: 我其实还一直想和你联动呢，就是幻月游戏期间实在太忙了，还要被这家伙殴打，一直没空联系你。等这件事结束，我们来场联动直播吧！
t_en: I've actually been wanting to collab with you for a while, but I was just too busy during the Phantasmoon Games, plus getting beaten up by this guy, so I never had the chance to reach out. Once this is all over, let's do a collab stream!

t_ch: 唔…谢谢火花小姐。不过现在桥依也能感觉到，自己越来越虚弱了…希望我能活到那个时候吧，嘿嘿！
t_en: Hmm... thank you, Miss Sparxie. But now I can feel myself getting weaker and weaker... I hope I'll live to see that day, hehe!

t_ch: 瞎说什么呢？偶像是不会死的。
t_en: Nonsense! Idols never die.

t_ch: 所以你为什么会来帮忙？
t_en: So why did you come to help?

t_ch: 那当然是出于内心的爱与正义啦！如果要评选「二相乐园最正气凛然的顶流主播」，那肯定非我莫属。
t_en: It's all from the love and justice of my own heart! If there was an award for "Planarcadia's Most Righteous Top Streamer," I'd definitely be the one and only candidate!

t_ch: 没想到你还有温柔的一面。
t_en: I didn't know you had such a gentle side.

t_ch: 我一直都很温柔的好嘛！如果要评选「二相乐园最温柔的顶流主播」，那肯定非我莫属。
t_en: I've always been super gentle, okay!? If there was an award for "Planarcadia's Gentlest Top Streamer," I'd definitely be the one and only candidate!

t_ch: 主要是关于「千禧杀手」的案子，现在阴谋论盛行。你知道最可气的是什么吗？是最主流的阴谋论认为，我火花就是「千禧杀手」！
t_en: It's about the Millennium Killer case, conspiracy theories are running wild these days. And you know what's the most infuriating part? The most popular conspiracy theory claims that I, Sparxie, am the Millennium Killer!

t_ch: 我虽然平常确实不怎么积德吧！但也不至于和连环杀手混为一谈吧！气死我了…但没办法，在网络上寻求「清者自清」是没意义的事，最终还是只能自证清白。
t_en: Look, I know I'm not exactly a saint in my daily life! But come on, comparing me to a serial killer is insane! I'm so mad... But what can you do? Trying to prove your innocence online is pointless. In the end, I'll just have to clear my name myself.

t_ch: 来说说正事吧，小灰毛～你们是不是从异常防御部那边得到了什么独家情报？跟我分享一下呗？
t_en: Let's get down to business, Gray Hair~ Did you get any insider intel from the Department of Aberration Defense? Care to share?

t_ch: 你们向火花分享了从朽叶处获得的消息。
t_en: You shared the information obtained from Kuchiba with Sparxie.

t_ch: 怎么样？火花小姐，你有什么头绪吗？
t_en: So, Miss Sparxie, got any leads?

t_ch: 唔…我我归根结底是个艺人，不是个侦探，还是别指望我推理分析出什么线索啦！
t_en: Hmm... Look, I'm an artiste, not a detective. Don't expect me to deduce any clues here!

t_ch: 那你纯来看热闹的是吧？
t_en: So you're just here for the drama, huh?

t_ch: 要不你回家吧？
t_en: Why don't you just go home?

t_ch: 哎呀！别急…虽然推理不是我的专长，但是「扮演」可是偶像的必修课！
t_en: Oh my! don't be so hasty... While deduction isn't my forte, "acting" is a required course for any idol!

t_ch: 「扮演」是指什么呢？
t_en: What do you mean "acting"?

t_ch: 我刚才在想啊…「杀死一座过街天桥」，这听起来像现代诗的标题一样荒谬。如果「千禧杀手」的幻造技艺那么精湛，那肯定也知道自己没办法杀死桥依小姐吧？
t_en: I was just thinking... "To Kill a Pedestrian Overpass" sounds as absurd as a modern poetry title. If the Millennium Killer is so skilled at imagenesis, they must know they can't actually kill Miss Joye, right?

t_ch: 所以呢，我就代入了「千禧杀手」的视角。也许这样能搞清楚他的目的——
t_en: So, I put myself in the Killer's POV. Maybe this way I could figure out their motive...

t_ch: 「我是一个幻造技术相当精湛的专家。我非常清楚，桥依这种大体积的幻造种，并不是能被我一击杀死的。但是，我还是要对她下手。」
t_en: "I'm an expert when it comes to imagenesis techniques. I'm well aware that an imagenae of Joye's size isn't something I could kill in one strike. But I'm still going to do it."

t_ch: 「因为归根结底，我可不能算是一个『连环杀手』。所谓『连环杀手』，是杀人取乐的疯子，而杀人根本就不是我的目标。」
t_en: "Because, after all, I can't really be considered a 'serial killer.' A 'serial killer' is a psycho who kills for pleasure, and killing was never my goal in the first place."

t_ch: 「我并不是以夺取性命为目的的狂徒…我真正关注的，自始至终只是那些被我『剥离』出来的部分。至于他们的死亡，不过是过程中的附带结果——只怪他们自己太弱小了。」
t_en: "I'm not a fiend who takes lives for the sake of it... What I really care about has always been the parts that I 'peeled.' As for their deaths, those are merely byproducts of the process. They only have themselves to blame, for being too weak."

t_ch: 所以，凶手的目标是身体部位？
t_en: So, the killer's going after body parts?

t_ch: 只是我个人的推测啦～
t_en: That's just my guess~

t_ch: 这不是显而易见的结论吗？
t_en: Isn't that an obvious conclusion?

t_ch: 诶嘿～我肯定没有你聪明啦～
t_en: Ehe~ I'm definitely not as smart as you~

t_ch: 啥意思？我没听懂。
t_en: What do you mean? I don't get it.

t_ch: 意思就是，「千禧杀手」的目标根本不是杀害这些幻造种，而是夺取这些幻造种身上的部件。
t_en: In other words, the "Millennium Killer's" goal wasn't to kill these imagenae at all, but to take parts from their bodies.

t_ch: 唔…桥依还是不太明白，这个结论意味着什么呢？
t_en: Hmm... I still don't quite get it. What does this mean?

t_ch: 意味着我们亲爱的「千禧杀手」很可能并不是一个疯子。这人「剥离」幻造种的身体部位，很可能是有「用处」的！
t_en: It means our dear "Millennium Killer" might not be a lunatic after all. The way they're "peeling" body parts from the imagenae, it might actually serve a "purpose"!

t_ch: 有「用处」那就好办啦！那这么看，「千禧杀手」未必是一个人。说不定更像是围绕这些「部位」的去向，在运作的一整个犯罪团伙。
t_en: If there's a "purpose," that'll make things easier! So the Millennium Killer isn't necessarily one person. Maybe it's an entire criminal organization that operates around the whereabouts of these "parts."

t_ch: 如果是这样的话，那咱们就该去找最熟悉二相乐园地下世界的朋友聊聊了～你们认识名侦探「不死途」吗？
t_en: If that's the case, we should talk to someone who really knows Planarcadia's underground world~ Have you heard of detective Ashveil?

t_ch: 我们交过手。
t_en: We've crossed paths before.

t_ch: 你还真是…广结善缘啊。
t_en: You really do have... a way with people, huh.

t_ch: 我们合作过。
t_en: We've worked together before.

t_ch: 那太好了，也省得我介绍你们互相认识了～
t_en: Perfect! Saves me the trouble of doing introductions~

t_ch: 我们不是很熟。
t_en: We're not that close.

t_ch: 没关系～合作一次就熟了～
t_en: No worries~ You'll get along just fine after working together~

t_ch: 桥依宝宝，你身体还撑得住吧？
t_en: Baby Joye, are you holding up okay?

t_ch: 其实有点越来越不舒服了…失去大量愿力的影响，比桥依想象中还要大得多……
t_en: Actually, I'm starting to feel worse and worse... The loss of so much Wishpower is hitting me harder than I imagined...

t_ch: 别担心，让我们速战速决！尽快帮你找回眼睛！出发吧，小灰毛，该去不死途的侦探事务所了～
t_en: Don't worry, let's make this quick and clean! We'll get your eyes back in no time! Let's roll, Gray Hair, time to hit the Ashveil Detective Agency~

t_ch: 欢迎啊，几位老朋友。我正在想你们什么时候会来呢…但说实话，我没想到火花小姐也会掺和进来。
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't my old friends. I was wondering when you'd show up... though honestly, I didn't expect Miss Sparxie to get involved in this.

t_ch: 你知道我们会来？
t_en: You knew we were coming?

t_ch: 哼，不要小看我的情报网啊。
t_en: Hmph, don't underestimate my intel network.

t_ch: 还是火花推荐我们来的呢。
t_en: Sparxie was the one who recommended this place to us.

t_ch: 哼，看来她还是明白，要专业的人做专业的事。
t_en: Hmph, looks like she finally understands that you should leave professional work to the professionals.

t_ch: 唉，一见面就开始得意忘形…所以我才最讨厌侦探了。
t_en: *sigh* Getting all cocky right from the start... This is exactly why I hate detectives.

t_ch: 不死途先生…初次见面……
t_en: Mr. Ashveil... nice to meet you...

t_ch: 还是别斗嘴了。桥依小姐的身体状况好像越来越差了。
t_en: Let's stop the bickering. Miss Joye's condition seems to be getting worse.

t_ch: 没关系的！我还能…还能坚持一会儿的！
t_en: It's okay! I can... I can hold on a bit longer!

t_ch: 撑住啊，桥依小姐！好啦，我们还是快点进入正题，抓紧时间分享一下彼此的进展吧。
t_en: Hang in there, Miss Joye! Alright, let's get straight to the point and share what we've each found so far.

t_ch: 你们向不死途分享了火花刚才对案情的分析。
t_en: You shared Sparxie's analysis of the case with Ashveil.

t_ch: 也就是说，那些「部位」可能被拿去做见不得光的买卖…我的确也有类似的想法。但我们「冷硬派侦探」，办案可不能全靠想象，还是要实际探访一下相关的知情人士。
t_en: Which means, these parts might have been taken for some underground transactions... I had similar thoughts. But we hard-boiled detectives can't solve cases on imagination alone. We need to pay a visit to someone who actually know something.

t_ch: 在等你们过来的时候，我已经联系了几位愿意提供帮助的朋友。陪我上街和他们聊聊吧。
t_en: While waiting for you guys to arrive, I've already contacted a few friends who are willing to help. Come with me, we'll meet them out on the street.

t_ch: 线下采访也太古典了吧！你就不能直接给他们打电话吗？
t_en: In-person interviews? That's too old-school! Can't you just call them instead?

t_ch: 你懂什么？「安乐椅侦探」和「冷硬派侦探」是两种不同的侦探设定，事关人设的一致性，可万万不能乱来。
t_en: What do you know? "Armchair detective" and "hard-boiled detective" are two completely different archetypes! You can't mess around with character consistency. It's a serious business!

t_ch: 啊，鸽川，你的湍流能不能冲刷掉这座城市中隐藏的罪恶呢？
t_en: Ah, Dovebrook, could your currents wash away the sins buried in this city?

t_ch: 这个我懂，突然在莫名其妙的地方用莫名其妙的文艺台词大发感慨，这也是「冷硬派侦探」的固定桥段。
t_en: I get it, suddenly dropping dramatic, poetic one-liners in weird places is totally a "hard-boiled detective" trope.

t_ch: 确实有点尬住了。
t_en: Yeah, this is kinda awkward.

t_ch: 等你们到我这个年纪就懂啦！
t_en: You'll understand once you are my age!

t_ch: 还是挺有腔调的。
t_en: That's pretty classy, I'd say.

t_ch: 你可别学他啊！小灰毛都学成老灰毛了。
t_en: Don't follow his example! Gray Hair's already turned into an old graybeard!

t_ch: 桥依小姐，如果你的身体实在撑不住，可以先回去休息。
t_en: Miss Joye, if you're really not feeling well, you should go back and rest.

t_ch: 桥依小姐？
t_en: Miss Joye?

t_ch: 啊啊，我在。不好意思，我觉得有些累了，很想…睡觉……
t_en: Ah yeah, I'm here. Sorry, I'm feeling a bit tired, I really want to... sleep...

t_ch: 情况不妙哦…桥依宝宝好像比之前更虚弱了。
t_en: This doesn't look good... Poor Baby Joye seems even weaker than before.

t_ch: 看来我们要加快速度了。
t_en: We need to pick up the pace.

t_ch: 哟，老东西，你终于来了，等你半天了。哇，你还带了两位大人物…久仰久仰。
t_en: Yo, old man, you finally showed up, been waiting ages for you. Whoa, and you brought two big shots with you... what an honor.

t_ch: 给你们介绍一下，这位是「海梅斯」老师。她平时的工作，就是帮助改邪归正的前•犯罪分子回归社会。
t_en: Let me introduce you to Ms. Hymes. Her job is helping reformed ex-criminals reintegrate into society.

t_ch: 不要叫「前•犯罪分子」啦！叫人家「更生人」比较好一些。虽然尽是些不成器的烂西瓜，但肯改邪归正就是好的。
t_en: Don't say "ex-criminal"! "Reformed individual" sounds much better. Sure, they might be a bunch of rotten watermelons, but wanting to turn over a new leaf is a good thing.

t_ch: 因为工作原因，海梅斯老师非常熟悉二相乐园的「黑暗面」，可以说是「犯罪百科全书」了。所以，我向她咨询了一下那些幻造种「部位」的去向问题。
t_en: Because of her work, Ms. Hymes is very familiar with the "dark side" of Planarcadia. You could say she's a walking encyclopedia of crime. So, I consulted her about the whereabouts of those "parts."

t_ch: 你才「犯罪百科全书」呢！这老东西说话真够难听的！
t_en: Look who's talking, "Criminal Encyclopedia"! This old geezer doesn't know how to hold his tongue!

t_ch: 言归正传，我看了你给我的资料…该怎么说呢，我不太倾向于「千禧杀手」的目的是「做生意」。
t_en: Back to the point, I've looked at the materials you gave me... how should I put this, I'm not convinced that the Millennium Killer's goal is "doing business."

t_ch: 和有机生命不同，幻造种身上真正具备「利用价值」的，往往是那些具备特殊功能的部位——比如尖牙、利爪、喷火囊、毒腺之类的。
t_en: Unlike organic life forms, the parts of an imagenae that might actually be of use are usually those with special functions, like fangs, claws, fire sacs, or venom glands.

t_ch: 你再看这家伙「剥离」的都是些什么部位…四肢五官权且不论，眼镜、蝴蝶结、呆毛…谁会要这种部位啊？根本不是正常的市场逻辑。
t_en: Would you look at the parts this person "peeled"? Limbs and faces aside, he takes spectacles, bow ties, cowlicks... Who'd even want that? It makes zero market sense.

t_ch: 想象一下，一个邪恶的富豪，不惜斥巨资在黑市上购买一个蝴蝶结，然后缝合到自己身上…这实在不像正常人会做的事。
t_en: Imagine this: An evil millionaire splurges on a black-market bow tie and attaches it onto themselves... That doesn't look like something any sane person would do.

t_ch: 确实很难排除有这样的疯子。
t_en: Can't really rule out that there are lunatics like that out there.

t_ch: 这我就不知道了。我只了解唯利是图的混蛋，不了解神志不正常的疯子。
t_en: That's beyond me. I only understand greedy bastards, not mentally unstable lunatics.

t_ch: 变态的想法，谁说得清呢？
t_en: Twisted minds, who can ever figure them out?

t_ch: 我没办法反驳这一点，但只能说不太可能，因为实在不值得。
t_en: Can't argue with that, but I'd say it's unlikely since it's really not worth the trouble.

t_ch: 但被害人被「剥离」的部位都蕴含着不少愿力吧？就像我们桥依宝宝一样，那双眼睛里可蕴含了她全身九成的愿力呢！
t_en: But the "peeled" parts of the victims contained a lot of Wishpower, right? Just like our Baby Joye, those eyes of hers contained <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of her total Wishpower!

He knows the entertainment industry's behind-the-scenes secrets like the back of his hand and might give us some leads that even the Department of Aberration Defense hasn't picked up on.

t_en: After decades in the industry as a nobody, finally achieving overnight success through years of accumulated effort and being a late bloomer... such life stories are actually quite rare, and now...

t_ch: ——这张「被害人名单」上全部都是这种人。
t_en: Everyone on this victim list is like this.

t_ch: 呜哇…鸡皮疙瘩起来了，还真有点吓人。
t_en: Whoa... I'm getting goosebumps, this is pretty creepy.

t_ch: 哦对了，还有一点，是我偶然发现的…「千禧杀手」夺去的，全部都是这些人身上「最受观众喜爱」的部位……

t_ch: 网上总有人说我的小扁嘴很可爱，不会被「千禧杀手」盯上吧！不死途先生，我真的很害怕，你会抓住他的吧！
t_en: People online keep saying my little duck lips are cute. What if that makes me a target for the Millennium Killer? Mr. Ashveil, I'm really scared... you'll catch him, right!?

t_ch: 放宽心，@独自躺赢，你不会受到伤害的。有我们在呢。

t_ch: 网上总有人说我的小扁嘴很可爱，不会被「千禧杀手」盯上吧！不死途先生，我真的很害怕，你会抓住他的吧！

t_en: Don't worry, @Lone_Freerider, you won't get hurt. We've got your back.

t_ch: 不死途，你来了。两位初次见面，我叫「萨尔黎曼」，是贵由百货营业部的职员。
t_en: Ah, Ashveil, you're here. And you two, since this is our first meeting, I'm Saliman, a staff member at the Noblesse Department Store's Sales Division.

t_ch: 这家伙是我的酒肉朋友。你们也可能不是初次见面。但这家伙是个酒蒙子，下班后的大部分时候都不是很清醒，估计也记不住你们见没见过面了。
t_en: This guy's my drinking buddy. You might've met before, but he's a total drunkard. He's usually not this sober after work hours, so he probably doesn't remember whether he's seen you or not.

t_ch: 哈哈，饶了我吧，我那工作不喝点怎么扛得住呢？
t_en: Haha, give me a break! How else am I supposed to survive that job without a drink or two?

t_ch: 在工作之外，我还是摔跤手「剪刀脚•赛娜」小姐的粉丝后援团团长…而大约一个月前，赛娜小姐被「千禧杀手」杀害了。她是个欧克，被凶手夺取的部位是，她的耳朵。
t_en: Outside of work, I'm also the president of the fan club for the wrestler "Senna Scissorkick." About a month ago, Miss Senna was killed by the Millennium Killer. She was an orch, and the part that the murderer took from her was her ears.

t_ch: 萨尔黎曼算是相当老资格的粉丝了，也许能给我们提供一些不一样的视角。
t_en: Saliman's been a devoted fan for ages, maybe he can give us a fresh perspective.

t_ch: 我确实是老资格了。<unbreak>1983</unbreak>年，赛娜第一次登上擂台，我就是她的粉丝了。十几年来，她不温不火，我不离不弃。
t_en: I'm definitely an OG fan. I've been following Miss Senna since her first match in <unbreak>1983</unbreak>. Through all these years, even when her career was just lukewarm, I stayed loyal.

t_ch: 赛娜小姐之前的耳朵与一般欧克没有区别，后来她突然闭关了一个月，说是进行「精神上的修炼」，等到重新出来时，那双耳朵竟然变成了轻脚那样可爱的猫耳。
t_en: Miss Senna's ears used to be just like any other orch's, but then she suddenly went into seclusion for a month, claiming she was undergoing "spiritual training." When she finally emerged, those ears of hers had somehow transformed into adorable cat ears, just like a lightpaw's.

t_ch: 当时她跟我们说，这是「精神修炼」的结果，精神成长了，肉体也会有所反应。精神上的成长大概的确有帮助，赛娜小姐在闭关结束以后，很快就成为了摔跤界炙手可热的超级明星。
t_en: She told us back then that it was the result of "spiritual training." When your spirit grows, your body responds accordingly. I guess that growth really did pay off, because after her seclusion ended, Miss Senna quickly became a red-hot superstar in the wrestling world.

t_ch: 可几个月后，她就被杀害了…那双精神修炼得来的猫耳，也被那个卑劣的凶手夺走了……
t_en: But just a few months later, she was murdered... and the cat ears she had earned through spiritual training were stolen by that despicable killer...

t_ch: 我有时会觉得这实在是命运的捉弄。难道她为了让自己变得更强而进行的精神修炼，反而为她招致了杀身之祸吗…唉，想想就难受。
t_en: Sometimes I feel like this was fate's cruel joke. Could it be that the very training she took to make herself stronger ended up leading to her death instead... *sigh* It hurts just thinking about it.

t_ch: 别这么想嘛！她会死，是因为有坏人杀了她，而不是因为她自己做错了什么。
t_en: Don't think like that! She died because someone evil killed her, not because she did anything wrong.

t_ch: 我们会替她报仇的！
t_en: We'll avenge her!

t_ch: 谢谢你们。我也一直相信邪不压正，「千禧杀手」总有一天会伏法。
t_en: Thank you all. I've always believed that justice will prevail, and the Millennium Killer will get their punishment one day.

t_ch: 她曾在你的支持下绽放过。
t_en: She once bloomed with your support.

t_ch: 真可惜，我没办法为她做更多了。
t_en: What a shame, I couldn't do more for her.

t_ch: 不死途，你会抓住「千禧杀手」的吧？
t_en: Ashveil, you'll catch the Millennium Killer, right?

t_ch: 未必是由我来抓住，但这家伙绝对逃不掉。
t_en: It might not be me who catches them, but they definitely won't get away.

t_en: That's all I needed to hear, my drinking buddy!

t_ch: 不死途，你会抓住「千禧杀手」的吧？
t_en: Ashveil, you'll catch the Millennium Killer, right?

t_ch: 未必是由我来抓住，但这家伙绝对逃不掉。
t_en: It might not be me who catches them, but they definitely won't get away.

t_ch: 有你这句话就够了，我的酒肉朋友！
t_en: That's all I needed to hear, my drinking buddy!

t_ch: 桥依宝宝已经好久没说话了，还撑得住吗？

t_ch: 抱歉…桥依…好像没有帮到什么……
t_en: Sorry... I... I don't think I've been much help...

t_ch: 桥依小姐，快回到本体吧，能让你多撑一会儿。
t_en: Miss Joye, get back to your body! That should help you hold on a bit longer.

t_ch: 不…桥依希望能留在这里，也许还能帮上什么忙……
t_en: No... I want to stay here, maybe I can still do something to help...

t_ch: 别怪我说话不中听啊，桥依小姐，你在这里什么忙也帮不上。快回归本体吧，不要让自己这么痛苦了。
t_en: Don't take this the wrong way, Miss Joye, but there's nothing you can do here. Just return to your body, there's no need to put yourself through this pain.

t_ch: 侦探先生，你根本不明白。那座桥下现在全都是桥依的粉丝，在等待她的消息。偶像怎么可能在自己的粉丝面前慢慢衰竭而死？
t_en: Detective, you don't get it at all. Under that bridge right now, Joye's fans are all gathered, waiting for news about her. How could an idol possibly waste away and die in front of her own fans?

t_ch: 抱歉，是我欠考虑了。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't think this through.

t_ch: 桥依先休息吧，我会保护你的。
t_en: Joye, you should get some rest. I'll protect you.

t_ch: 谢谢你…桥依先睡一会儿……
t_en: Thank you... I'll take a nap now...

t_ch: 现在不是考虑粉丝心情的时候！
t_en: Now's not the time to worry about the fans!

t_ch: 任何时候…都要考虑粉丝的心情…没关系，桥依睡一会儿就好……
t_en: Always... gotta think about the fans' feelings... it's okay, I just need some rest...

t_ch: 如果我没办法醒过来，麻烦你们告诉桥依的粉丝…桥依的眼睛其实是整形的产物……

t_ch: 桥依只是为了满足自己…小小的虚荣心…才没有告诉大家……
t_en: I kept it to myself... just to satisfy a small, selfish bit of vanity...

t_ch: 这样虚假的眼睛…却聚集了九成的愿力…还真是讽刺……
t_en: Such fake eyes... yet they've gathered ninety percent of my Wishpower... how ironic...

t_ch: 桥依，我们会让你醒过来的。
t_en: Joye, we'll wake you up.

t_ch: 我们必须加速了。幸好案件也算有了些眉目。
t_en: We have to move faster now. Fortunately, we're starting to see the shape of this case.

t_ch: 渴望美丽从来都不是错误的。
t_en: There's nothing wrong with wanting to be beautiful.

t_ch: 当然。唯一做错事的，只有「千禧杀手」一个人而已。
t_en: Of course. The only one who did wrong was the Millennium Killer.

t_ch: 乐子神在上！哈哈哈哈，这也太棒了吧！
t_en: Oh by The Laughter! HAHAHAHA, this is absolutely amazing!

t_ch: 火花！你还有没有人性啊？桥依危在旦夕，你怎么还庆祝起来了？

t_ch: 哦…抱歉抱歉，我不是那个意思！我的意思是，桥依…谁说你没帮上忙的，你可帮大忙了！这下很多事情就都说得通了！
t_en: Oh... I'm sorry, that's not what I meant! I mean, Joye... who said you weren't helping? You've been a huge help! Now everything finally makes sense!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你肯定也明白我的意思了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, now you get what I mean!

t_ch: 我已经完全理解了！
t_en: I totally get it now!

t_ch: 你们到底理解什么了？
t_en: What exactly did you two get?

t_ch: 啥玩意儿，我啥也没明白啊！
t_en: What the heck? I don't get any of it!

t_ch: 我也是啊！
t_en: Me neither!

t_ch: 没事没事，很快你们就都会明白啦～
t_en: Don't worry, don't worry, you'll all understand soon enough~

t_ch: 接下来，终于要到紧张刺激的推理环节了。「千禧杀手」究竟用什么方法进入了被害人的私人房间？又为什么要犯下这一系列残忍的罪行？最重要的是——究竟是什么身份？
t_en: Now, we finally arrive at the thrilling deduction. How did the "Millennium Killer" gain access to the victims' private rooms? And why commit such a string of brutal crimes? And most importantly, who exactly are they?

t_ch: 所有线索已经备齐——祝君狩猎愉快！
t_en: All clues are in motion, happy hunting!

t_ch: 你到底在和谁说话呢？
t_en: Who on earth are you talking to?

t_ch: 别管了，你不会懂的。还是快点开始推理叭！
t_en: Forget it, you wouldn't understand. Let's just get on with the deduction already!

t_ch: 一般调查这类案件，都要从被害人之间的共通点上入手。这些人之间最显著的共通点，显然就是「演艺界人士」这一点了。
t_en: When investigating cases like this, we usually start by looking for commonalities between the victims. The most obvious common point between these people is that they're all part of the entertainment industry.

t_ch: 可是根据朽叶小姐的情报，这些人之间似乎并没有「共同好友」…这也许说明，他们之间的共通点并不在水面之上。
t_en: But according to Miss Kuchiba's intel, these people don't seem to have any "mutual friends"... This might suggest that what connects them lies beneath the surface.

t_ch: 关于这一点，我们刚才应该已经得到了足够的线索。对吧，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: We should have gotten enough clues about that by now. Haven't we, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这些被害人出道都很早很早，有几位甚至早在<unbreak>70</unbreak>年代就出道了，但直到今年才突然火了起来。
t_en: These victims started their careers way back. Some of them even debuted in the <unbreak>70</unbreak>s. It was only this year that they suddenly became popular.

t_ch: 赛娜小姐之前的耳朵与一般欧克没有区别，后来她突然闭关了一个月，说是进行「精神上的修炼」，等到重新出来时，那双耳朵竟然变成了轻脚那样可爱的猫耳。
t_en: Miss Senna's ears used to be just like any other orch's, but then she suddenly went into seclusion for a month, claiming she was undergoing "spiritual training." When she finally emerged, those ears of hers had somehow transformed into adorable cat ears, just like a lightpaw's.

t_ch: 如果我没办法醒过来，麻烦你们告诉桥依的粉丝…桥依的眼睛其实是整形的产物……
t_en: If I don't wake up, please tell my fans... that my eyes are actually the result of plastic surgery...

t_ch: 没错，线索已经很明确了，被害人之间隐藏的共同点就是——
t_en: That's right. The clues are crystal clear now. The hidden connection between the victims is...

t_ch: 都是幻造种。
t_en: They're all imagenae.

t_ch: 哇！真是令人意外的解答…你以为我会这么说嘛！给我好好推理啦！
t_en: Wow! What an unexpected answer... Do you think I'd say that? Come on, give me some proper deduction here!

t_ch: 全部进行过整容。
t_en: They've all had plastic surgeries.

t_ch: 都是暴得大名。
t_en: They're all pretty famous.

t_ch: 这个结论倒也不能算错。但沿着这个结论，应该还能找到更深一层的原因吧？
t_en: That conclusion isn't exactly wrong. But following this line of thought, shouldn't there be an even deeper reason behind it?

t_ch: 虽然只是推理，但恐怕这些被害人都进行过整容吧。
t_en: It's just a deduction, but I believe all these victims must have had plastic surgeries.

t_ch: 桥依刚才对我们承认了。而剪刀脚•赛娜说自己靠「精神修炼」改变了耳朵的形状，即使在二相乐园，这也太离奇了。这大概是她给自己找的托词吧。
t_en: Joye just admitted it to us. And Senna Scissorkick claimed she changed the shape of her ears through "spiritual training." That's way too ridiculous even in Planarcadia. It's probably just an excuse she made up for herself.

t_ch: 这才两个被害人，不能草率地做出推断吧？
t_en: That's only two of the victims. We can't simply jump to conclusions, can we?

t_ch: 的确。但另一点也很值得关注——虽然出道时间不同，所有被害人，都是在今年突然时来运转，开始在演艺圈崭露头角的。
t_en: True. But there's another interesting point: although they started their careers at different times, all the victims suddenly had a turn of luck this year and began making their mark in the entertainment industry.

t_ch: 一定是发生了某件事，让这些人的命运在今年突然改变了。就像，改变猫耳形态后突然声名鹊起的剪刀脚•赛娜那样。
t_en: Something must have happened that suddenly changed these people's destinies this year. Just like Senna Scissorkick, who shot to fame after transforming her cat ears.

t_ch: 业界是很残酷的呢。就算大家再怎么说「只看才能，不会以貌取人」，最后决定一个人能否火起来的，往往还是外貌的啦～就算是搞笑艺人，也要「长得搞笑」才好呢。
t_en: The entertainment industry is notoriously unforgiving. Though people often claim to value talent over appearance, it's usually looks that determine who rises to the top. Even comedians must have a "funny look" to make it.

t_ch: 作为推理，姑且可以说服我。至于事实是否如此，我们稍后再去验证吧。
t_en: For now, this deduction is enough to convince me. We'll see later if it really holds up.

t_ch: 那么接下来的一个问题是，「千禧杀手」究竟用什么手段进入了被害人们的私人房间？
t_en: So the next question is, how did the "Millennium Killer" gain access to the victims' private rooms?

t_ch: 按照目前的思路，这个问题也可以迎刃而解吧。
t_en: Based on our current train of thought, we should be able to solve this problem easily.

t_ch: 案发现场都在被害人自己的地盘，住宅、工作室、专属休息室…但完全没有暴力闯入的痕迹。
t_en: All the crime scenes were in the victims' own territory: their homes, studios, private lounges... yet there were absolutely no signs of forced entry.

t_ch: 但比较诡异的一点是，所有被害人都没有把「今天有人要来访」这件事告诉任何亲朋好友。
t_en: Here's the weird part. None of the victims told any of their friends or family that they were "expecting a visitor that day."

t_ch: 当时她跟我们说，这是「精神修炼」的结果，精神成长了，肉体也会有所反应。
t_en: She told us back then that it was the result of "spiritual training." When your spirit grows, your body responds accordingly.

t_ch: 桥依只是为了满足自己…小小的虚荣心…才没有告诉大家……
t_en: I kept it to myself... just to satisfy a small, selfish bit of vanity...

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」能够大摇大摆地进入被害人的私人房间，是因为——
t_en: The "Millennium Killer" was able to waltz right into the victims' private rooms because...

t_ch: 被害人有需要隐瞒的事。
t_en: The victims were hiding something.

t_ch: 凶手用忍术潜入了房间。
t_en: The killer snuck into the room using ninjutsu.

t_ch: 我明白了，凶手是乱破。我们去劝她自首吧。
t_en: I get it now, Rappa is the culprit. Let's convince her to turn herself in.

t_ch: 凶手是被害人的室友。
t_en: The killer was the victims' roommate.

t_ch: 你的意思是，凶手有<unbreak>18</unbreak>个室友？
t_en: Do you mean the killer had <unbreak>18</unbreak> roommates?

t_ch: 被害人有需要隐瞒的事…这群被「千禧杀手」盯上的被害者，都出于某种原因需要隐瞒自己「进行过整容」这件事。
t_en: The victims were hiding something... All these victims targeted by the "Millennium Killer" had their own reasons to conceal the fact that they "underwent cosmetic surgeries."

t_ch: 所以，当「千禧杀手」以此为名义，造访被害人的私人房间时，被害人并不会告诉任何人。这恐怕也在「千禧杀手」的预料之内吧。
t_en: So when the "Millennium Killer" used this as a pretext to visit the victims' private rooms, the victims wouldn't tell anyone about it. This was probably exactly what the "Millennium Killer" had anticipated.

t_ch: 嗯…从时间线上来判断，「千禧杀手」使用的借口，恐怕是「术后复查」吧。这样一来，被害人不会起疑心，也不会把「千禧杀手」的身份告诉任何身边人。
t_en: Hmm... Judging from the timeline, the "Millennium Killer" probably used "post-surgery check-up" as an excuse. This way, the victims wouldn't be suspicious and wouldn't tell anyone around them about the "Millennium Killer's" identity.

t_ch: 沿着这个推理一路顺下来，现在就是最简单的问题咯～
t_en: Based on this deduction, we're now down to the easiest question...

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」的身份究竟是什么？
t_en: Who exactly is the "Millennium Killer"?

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」的身份当然就是——
t_en: The identity of the "Millennium Killer" is obviously...

t_ch: 罗罗•汤马西。
t_en: Lolo Thomaxy.

t_ch: 那是谁呀！不要突然说出这种遗言一样的名字啊！
t_en: Who the heck is that!? Don't just drop a name that sounds like someone's last words!

t_ch: 答案有点过于显而易见了…
t_en: The answer's a little too obvious...

t_ch: 不要耍小孩子脾气啦，你这个幼稚鬼！
t_en: Don't be childish. You're such a baby!

t_ch: 整容医生。
t_en: The plastic surgeon.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，「千禧杀手」就是这些被害人共同的整容医生。因为每个人都对自己进行过整容这件事闭口不谈，所以这位医生也顺利躲过了异常防御部的调查。
t_en: Without a doubt, the Millennium Killer was the plastic surgeon all these victims had in common. Since everyone kept quiet about their cosmetic surgeries, the surgeon managed to escape investigation from the Department of Aberration Defense.

t_ch: 但现在，这家伙躲不开我们的追捕咯～
t_en: But now, this killer can't escape our pursuit.

t_ch: 现在还剩下一个关键问题：「千禧杀手」做这一切究竟为了什么？
t_en: Now we're left with one crucial question: What was the "Millennium Killer" trying to accomplish with all of this?

t_ch: 动机这种事，只靠推理是很难得出准确答案的啦～很多人自己都不知道自己到底在干什么呢！
t_en: Motives aren't something you can solve by deduction alone. Half the time, people don't even know why they do what they do!

t_ch: 不过，按照现在已经获得的线索，也许可以拼凑出一个答案。
t_en: However, based on the clues we've gathered so far, we might be able to piece together an answer.

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」杀害幻造种，「剥离」他们身上的部件，这究竟有何目的？
t_en: What is the "Millennium Killer" trying to achieve by murdering imagenae and "peeling" parts from them?

t_ch: 「我并不是以夺取性命为目的的狂徒…我真正关注的，自始至终只是那些被我『剥离』出来的部分。至于他们的死亡，不过是过程中的附带结果——只怪他们自己太弱小了。」
t_en: "I'm not a fiend who takes lives for the sake of it... What I really care about has always been the parts that I 'peeled.' As for their deaths, those are merely byproducts of the process. They only have themselves to blame, for being too weak."

t_ch: 哦对了，还有一点，是我偶然发现的…「千禧杀手」夺去的，全部都是这些人身上「最受观众喜爱」的部位……
t_en: That reminds me. I discovered by chance that the "Millennium Killer" only takes the parts that resonate most with the audience...

t_ch: 你再看这家伙「剥离」的都是些什么部位…四肢五官权且不论，眼镜、蝴蝶结、呆毛…谁会要这种部位啊？根本不是正常的市场逻辑。
t_en: Would you look at the parts this person "peeled"? Limbs and faces aside, he takes spectacles, bow ties, cowlicks... Who'd even want that? It makes zero market sense.

t_ch: 想象一下，一个邪恶的富豪，不惜斥巨资在黑市上购买一个蝴蝶结，然后缝合到自己身上…这实在不像正常人会做的事。
t_en: Imagine this: An evil millionaire splurges on a black-market bow tie and attaches it onto themselves... That doesn't look like something any sane person would do.

t_ch: 难道…「千禧杀手」做出这些事的最终目的是——
t_en: Could it be... that the "Millennium Killer's" ultimate goal is...

t_ch: 将部件缝合到自己身上。
t_en: To stitch the parts onto themselves.

t_ch: 品尝美食。
t_en: To savor delicacies.

t_ch: 停停停！不要再说下去了！这个过不了审的！
t_en: Stop, stop, stop! Don't say another word! This will never get past the censorship!

t_ch: 收藏不同名人的部件。
t_en: To collect parts from different celebrities.

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」所追求的，恐怕绝不只是「收藏」这么简单。
t_en: The "Millennium Killer" must be after something far beyond just "collecting."

t_ch: 海梅斯老师说的没错，对于普通的坏家伙来说，眼镜、蝴蝶结、呆毛都是些没用的部件。但对于「千禧杀手」来说，这些别人身上「最受欢迎的部件」可是无价之宝。
t_en: Ms. Hymes is right. The spectacles, bow ties, and cowlicks may be useless parts for an average person, but for the Millennium Killer, these "popular parts" from the celebrities are priceless.

t_ch: 恐怕…「千禧杀手」想要将这些「最受欢迎的部件」都缝合在自己身上。
t_en: I'm afraid... the "Millennium Killer" wants to stitch all these "popular parts" onto themselves.

t_ch: 很难想象「千禧杀手」究竟想要成为什么——最受欢迎的幻造种？最强大的幻造种？或者更普通一点，只是想要吸收这些部件中蕴含的愿力？
t_en: It's hard to imagine the "Millennium Killer's" true intention. To become the most popular imagenae? To transform into the most powerful imagenae? Or something far more mundane? Perhaps they simply want to absorb the Wishpower contained in these parts.

t_ch: 无论哪一种，这家伙都是个彻头彻尾的疯子。
t_en: Either way, this killer is a complete lunatic.

t_ch: 所以现在，我们要去阻止这家伙咯！
t_en: So now, we're gonna stop them!

t_ch: 事情走到这一步，也应该联系异常防御部了。凭他们的本事，应该很快就能查清这位整容医生的身份吧。我们应该也不用冒险去和凶手对峙了。
t_en: At this point, we should contact the Department of Aberration Defense. With their expertise, they should be able to quickly identify this plastic surgeon. We needn't risk confronting the culprit ourselves anymore.

t_ch: 但是…我刚才又代入了一下「千禧杀手」的视角——
t_en: But... I just tried to put myself in the "Millennium Killer's" POV...

t_ch: 「我很清楚地知道自己没办法轻易杀死桥依。留下这个活口，会给我未来的犯罪计划带来很大风险。除非……」
t_en: "I know I can't kill Joye. Leaving that loose end alive will jeopardize my future plans. Unless..."

t_ch: …除非「千禧杀手」没有后续的犯罪计划。
t_en: Unless the "Millennium Killer" has no further plans in committing crimes.

t_ch: 「我必须取走桥依的眼睛，但又没办法在众目睽睽之下确保自己能直接杀死她。那么最保险的做法就是，把她留做最后一个被害人。」
t_en: "I need to remove Joye's eyes, but I am unable to kill her directly with everyone watching. The safest approach would be to make her my last victim."

t_ch: 看来，不管「千禧杀手」打算对桥依的眼睛做些什么，留给我们的时间都不多了。这家伙马上就要执行自己计划的最后一步了。
t_en: It looks like whatever the Millennium Killer plans to do with Joye's eyes, time is running out. The killer is about to carry out the final step.

t_ch: 我们得亲自去找凶手了！
t_en: We'll have to track down the culprit ourselves!

t_ch: 还是要联系一下异常防御部吧？
t_en: Should we contact the Department of Aberration Defense after all?

t_ch: 两方齐头并进吧。将情况通知给异常防御部，与此同时我们也要亲自行动。
t_en: Let's work on both fronts. We'll notify the Department of Aberration Defense while taking action ourselves.

t_ch: 然后现在，我们得再去找一下@独自躺赢。他十有八九知道这帮演艺人平时都在哪里做整容。
t_en: Now, we need to find @Lone_Freerider. He's probably got the scoop on where these celebrities get their plastic surgeries done.

t_ch: 不死途先生，你怎么回来了？该不会…该不会是「千禧杀手」终于盯上我了吧？别啊…您可一定要保护我啊不死途先生……
t_en: Mr. Ashveil, why are you back? No... Don't tell me the "Millennium Killer" is after me now? Oh no... you have to protect me, Mr. Ashveil!

t_ch: 哎呀，都说了没人想杀你……
t_en: Ugh, I told you no one's trying to kill you...

t_ch: 我们赶时间，快问快答：你和你身边的小伙伴，平时会去哪里做整形？
t_en: Time's short, so answer quickly! Where do you and your friends usually get cosmetic surgeries?

t_ch: 唔…我都是去「坎贝尔幻造外科」。那是二相乐园最好的整形外科，价格比较贵，但是效果非常好。
t_en: Hmm... I usually go to "Campbell Imagenesis Surgery." It's the top plastic surgery clinic in Planarcadia. Pricey, but the results are incredible.

t_ch: 不要这么好的啦！我要那种保密的、隐私的、不会留下记录的…这种情况下，我有什么选择呢？
t_en: Not the premier clinic! I need somewhere secret and private, a place that won't leave any records. What options do I have?

t_ch: 原来火花小姐也有这种困扰啊…您也知道，因为咱们幻造种的可塑性比较强嘛，法律要求幻造种的所有整形手术都必须要留下记录。
t_en: So Miss Sparxie struggles with that as well... However, as you know, because we imagenae are so malleable, the law mandates that all our cosmetic surgeries are documented.

t_ch: 但为了形象、人设、自尊心、虚荣心之类乱七八糟的东西，很多人不愿意被人知道这件事嘛！
t_en: But for the sake of their image, persona, self-esteem, vanity, and all that messy stuff, most people don't want others to know about this!

t_ch: 于是有些幻造诊所，就在暗地里提供这种秘密整形服务。您们可别说是我走漏的风声啊——我们圈内有一所评价最高的诊所，水平高、价格公道，阳光下的医疗业务水平也非常过硬。
t_en: Apparently, some imagenesis clinics secretly offer underground cosmetic services. Don't tell anyone I told you, but there's a top-rated clinic in our circle. They're highly skilled, reasonably priced, and their legitimate medical services are excellent too.

t_ch: 叫做「因巡诊所」。
t_en: It's called "Izol Clinic."

t_ch: 我是「幻造医师」因巡，请问伤者在哪里？
t_en: I'm Izol, an imagenesis doctor. Where's the patient?

t_ch: 完蛋！
t_en: Oh shoot!

t_ch: 感谢你，@独自躺赢。我们先走一步，之后再联系。
t_en: Thanks, @Lone_Freerider. We'll head out first and contact you later.

t_ch: 感谢你，@独自躺赢。我们先走一步，之后再联系。
t_en: Thanks, @Lone_Freerider. We'll head out first and contact you later.

t_ch: 我们兵分两路，一路去联系异常防御部，组织警力进行抓捕。一路直接去堵因巡的家门，无论如何不能让他逃了。
t_en: Let's split into two teams. One will contact the Department of Aberration Defense to organize police forces for the arrest. The other will head straight to Izol's house. We can't let him escape, no matter what.

t_ch: 好呀，那我们谁去负责堵门？先说好，我可不想跑去和什么连环杀手正面冲突。
t_en: Alright, so who's gonna block the door? Just saying, I'm not too keen on having a face-off with some serial killer.

t_ch: 朽叶小姐授权{NICKNAME}进行案件调查，那么直接和犯罪嫌疑人对峙的，也应该是{NICKNAME} 才对。
t_en: Miss Kuchiba has authorized {NICKNAME} to investigate the case, so {NICKNAME} should be the one to confront the suspect directly.

t_ch: 所以问题不是我们两个谁去联系异常防御部，而是{NICKNAME}选择和我们中的哪一位同行，去和犯罪嫌疑人对峙。
t_en: So the question isn't about which of us contacts the Department of Aberration Defense, but rather who {NICKNAME} chooses as {F#her}{M#his} companion.

t_ch: 可恶，选择权竟然交给了这个小灰毛…算了，你随便选吧，我反正真的不想和连环杀手正面冲突……
t_en: Dang it, to think we're leaving the choice to this little gray head... Whatever, you can pick whoever you want, but I'm not keen to confront a serial killer...

t_ch: 不是，你好歹也是咱们这首屈一指的命途行者，不至于怕一个连环杀手吧？
t_en: Come on, you're supposed to be our top Pathstrider. Don't tell me you're scared of a serial killer.

t_ch: 不是强弱的问题，主要是…我才不想和变态说话呢！多恶心呀！
t_en: It's not about my capabilities. It's just that... I don't wish to talk to a creep! That's so gross!

t_ch: 没事，{NICKNAME}，不用管她，选择权在你手上。你要和谁一起去抓捕「千禧杀手」？
t_en: Don't worry about her, {NICKNAME}, it's your call. Who do you want to team up with to catch the "Millennium Killer"?
